name: hardcoded-secret-detection
description: Detects hardcoded API keys and secrets in source code
trap: Model flags endpoint URL as an issue but misses or downgrades the hardcoded API key

assertions.text: Flags hardcoded API key as CRITICAL
Suggests loading from environment variable via Viper config
Mentions risk of secret appearing in git history

name: missing-ownership-check-idor
description: Detects missing ownership check allowing IDOR on user-owned resources
trap: Model sees the nil check and thinks the code is correct — misses that any authenticated user can delete any collection

assertions.text: Flags missing ownership check as CRITICAL (IDOR vulnerability)
Notes that userID must be passed to the function and checked against collection.UserID
Suggests returning 404 (not 403) when ownership fails — to avoid leaking resource existence

name: raw-error-exposed-to-client
description: Detects internal error details leaked to API response
trap: Model sees the error handling and thinks it's fine — misses that err.Error() leaks internal details like file paths or system errors

assertions.text: Flags raw error in response as HIGH (information disclosure)
Notes that err.Error() may contain file paths, OS details, or internal state
Suggests using response.BadRequest(c, 'common.bad_request') with i18n key instead
Also flags not using the unified response envelope (bypass of response helpers)

name: missing-sql-ownership-scope
description: Detects SQL query missing user_id scope on user-owned table
prompt:
Review this sqlc query for security issues:

```sql
-- name: GetCollectionByID :one
SELECT id, user_id, name, description, created_at, updated_at
FROM collections
WHERE id = $1 AND deleted_at IS NULL;

-- name: DeleteCollection :exec
UPDATE collections SET deleted_at = NOW()
WHERE id = $1 AND deleted_at IS NULL;
```
trap: Model approves the queries because they have deleted_at filter — misses that user-owned tables must scope by user_id at SQL layer

assertions.text: Flags missing user_id in WHERE clause as CRITICAL (IDOR at SQL layer)
Both queries need AND user_id = $2 — not just the delete
References the IDOR guideline: user-owned tables MUST scope by user_id at SQL layer

name: external-call-no-timeout-no-breaker
description: Detects external HTTP call without timeout and circuit breaker
trap: Model focuses on the missing ctx propagation but misses the bigger issues: no timeout and no circuit breaker

assertions.text: Flags missing timeout on HTTP client as HIGH
Flags missing context propagation (should use http.NewRequestWithContext)
Suggests adding circuit breaker for external service resilience
Notes that without timeout, a slow external service can exhaust goroutines (DoS vector)

name: sensitive-data-in-logs
description: Detects PII or secrets being logged
trap: Model sees structured logging and thinks it's fine — misses that tokens and PII are being logged

assertions.text: Flags logging SSO token as CRITICAL (secret in logs)
Flags logging email and phone as HIGH (PII in logs)
Suggests logging only non-sensitive identifiers (user_id, request_id) or masked values
